stem: A model predicts that the population of the town of Halden was 22,000 in 2010. The model also predicts that each year for the next 6 years, the population p increased by 3% of the previous year's population. Which equation best represents this model, where x is the number of years after 2010, for x ≤ 6?
rationale: Choice B is correct. The population grows by 3% per year, so each year the population is multiplied by 1.03. Starting from 22,000 in 2010, after x years the population is 22,000(1.03)x. Choices A and C raise 22,000 itself to the power x, which is not a valid exponential growth model. Choice D models a 3% annual decrease.
